basics.name: Junteng Liu
basics.summary: First-year Ph.D. candidate at HKUST NLP Group, focusing on natural language processing and machine learning. Research interests include LLM reasoning and reinforcement learning, hallucination in Vision-Language Models, and LLM truthfulness and interpretability.

work.summary: Research internship focused on NLP and machine learning
Research internship focused on NLP research
Research internship focused on machine learning and NLP

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST)

publications.name: Composing Parameter-Efficient Modules with Arithmetic Operations
publications.summary: Exploring composition of parameter-efficient modules using arithmetic operations for efficient model adaptation.

publications.name: C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models
publications.summary: Comprehensive evaluation suite with over 13,000 questions spanning 52 subjects across four categories.

publications.name: In-Context Sharpness as Alerts: An Inner Representation Perspective for Hallucination Mitigation
publications.summary: Introduces in-context sharpness as a novel metric for detecting and mitigating hallucinations in LLMs.

publications.name: On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs
publications.summary: Discovers a universal truthfulness hyperplane inside LLMs and explores its implications for LLM behavior.

publications.name: On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding
publications.summary: Investigates the perception bottleneck of Vision-Language Models in chart understanding tasks.

publications.name: SynLogic: Synthesizing Verifiable Reasoning Data at Scale for Learning Logical Reasoning and Beyond
publications.summary: Synthesizes verifiable reasoning data at scale for training models on logical reasoning and beyond.
